entry.title: A look at SHRINCS
entry.summary:
The ongoing development of the SHRINCS cryptographic scheme for Bitcoin is undergoing significant modifications to better cater to various use-cases. The original version lacked flexibility in its stateful signing path, which limited its applicability across different scenarios. However, the new design introduces a flexible XMSS structure that allows signers to choose between a balanced or unbalanced tree structure. This flexibility extends to the consensus-verifier, which is designed to accept signatures from either format without distinction. Although the concept of multi-tree XMSS support remains under consideration, it is primarily relevant for applications like lightning networks where extensive signing is necessary. If multiple stateful trees become too cumbersome, users might opt for the stateless path instead.
From a technical standpoint, improvements in signature efficiency and hardware compatibility are also being addressed. SPHINCS+ signatures can achieve a size of 3-5 kilobytes with proper parameter adjustments, offering a balance between security and performance. Moreover, RAM usage can be minimized using streaming techniques, and signing times can be drastically reduced—achieving speeds of 10 to 12 milliseconds on typical desktop CPUs, and even faster with GPU acceleration. However, challenges persist with hardware wallets and embedded systems, where current technologies result in longer signing times.
Additionally, there are considerations against fragmenting SHRINCS into separate components within Bitcoin's scripting language due to potential security risks in multisignature setups. A unified approach is favored to maintain simplicity and security in script implementations. An opcode for merkle branch checking has been suggested, possibly aligning with the OP_PAIRCOMMIT proposal, although this area requires further exploration.
These ongoing developments and discussions are crucial as they shape the feasibility and security of implementing advanced cryptographic schemes within Bitcoin’s ecosystem. The community continues to refine these technologies, ensuring they meet the needs of diverse applications while maintaining robust security standards. Updates and further specifications will be published both on [DelvingBitcoin](https://www.delvingbitcoin.org) and through other appropriate channels as the project progresses.
